title: Ervatamia merrilliana
intro: Ervatamia merrilliana, also known as the Philippine evratamia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ervatamia merrilliana include alkaloids, such as evratamin, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antitumor activities. In traditional medicine, Ervatamia merrilliana is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to make a variety of herbal teas and tonics.
taxonomy: Ervatamia merrilliana is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which includes a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family consists of over 5,000 species, with many of them being vines, shrubs, or trees. The genus Ervatamia is further divided into several species, with Ervatamia merrilliana being one of them. Ervatamia merrilliana is native to the tropical regions of Asia, and it is a climbing plant with a woody stem. The classification of Ervatamia merrilliana into the family Apocynaceae reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
